hostels_and_facilities.hostel_allotment_policy: All girls and PwD students are to receive on-campus hostel accommodation. Approximately 80-120 boys may need empanelled PG accommodation within about 6.5 km; on-campus space may become available from the third semester subject to availability.
hostels_and_facilities.mess: Hostel and institute mess/canteen serve vegetarian and non-vegetarian options at fixed timings.
hostels_and_facilities.wifi: 1 Gbps NKN-backed campus LAN and Wi-Fi, available 24x7.
hostels_and_facilities.gym: Modern gymnasium in the Student Activity Centre for boys and girls.
hostels_and_facilities.library: 3,089 printed volumes, approximately 13,196 online journals through ONOS, 158-seat AC reading hall, published hours 8 AM-2 AM.
hostels_and_facilities.laboratories: Modern CSE and ECE laboratories, including high-performance servers and GPU infrastructure; 24x7 access is institution-published for computing facilities.
hostels_and_facilities.medical_centre: Official health-care page exists, but current staffing and emergency coverage require manual confirmation.
hostels_and_facilities.counselling_centre: Professional mental-wellness counselling twice weekly on campus and 24x7 online, institution-published.
hostels_and_facilities.transport: App-based taxis from airport/rail stations; some city buses reach the campus approach road. Empanelled PG travel is about 15-20 minutes by bus/car.
hostels_and_facilities.shops: Student Activity Centre and canteen facilities; nearby external amenities require verification.
hostels_and_facilities.accessibility: PwD hostel accommodation guaranteed; detailed accessibility audit unavailable.
hostels_and_facilities.security: Hostels have wardens and regulated entry/exit; older facilities page reports 24x7 security and power backup.
hostels_and_facilities.curfew: Exact official hostel curfew not published in the current profile; SAC access is stated until 11 PM and entry/exit timings are regulated.
hostels_and_facilities.anti_ragging: Official anti-ragging system and national helpline available.
hostels_and_facilities.grievance: Institute grievance contacts and government anti-ragging portal are available.

location.college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology Guwahati
location.technology_ecosystem: Guwahati regional technology and startup ecosystem; official collaborations include STPI and AMTRON
location.data_origin: Official JoSAA connectivity disclosure plus limited inference

senior_junior_culture.summary: Public evidence is too limited to generalize. Recent sources do not provide enough independent, detailed accounts of mentorship, hierarchy or fresher inclusion.
positive_themes: Official alumni platform and talks create potential mentoring channels
negative_themes: No reliable repeated public evidence on everyday senior-junior interaction

academic_culture.summary: A recent detailed anonymous account describes knowledgeable faculty but a competitive and tiring 9-to-5 weekday schedule. Older comparison threads also characterize academics as a relative strength. Official rules historically state a 75% attendance threshold.
positive_themes: Faculty guidance and academic quality are repeatedly viewed as strengths
Strong research and project opportunities are institution-published
negative_themes: Workload and competitive pace may feel demanding
Attendance flexibility should be verified from current regulations

hostel_reality.summary: Official 2026 data confirms multiple room types, regulated entry/exit and a possibility that 80-120 boys may use empanelled PG accommodation in first year. Student-source evidence on room and mess quality is limited and mixed.
positive_themes: On-campus accommodation guaranteed for girls and PwD students
Campus Wi-Fi, power backup and nearby SAC are officially described
negative_themes: Some first-year boys may live up to about 6.5 km away
Mess and room-quality evidence is too limited for a reliable quality judgment

club_and_campus_life.summary: Officially, 19 clubs and several fests operate under the Gymkhana Council. Public student discussions, however, repeatedly describe campus life or fest quality as limited compared with larger institutions.
positive_themes: Current official profile lists technical, cultural, sports and welfare clubs
Entropy, Yuvaan, Invicta, Sankalp and entrepreneurship events provide formal opportunities
negative_themes: Several public accounts describe quieter or weaker campus life
Club-specific recent activity is not well documented publicly

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Official placement outcomes and a current Programming Club/ML/robotics developer ecosystem indicate meaningful technical opportunity. Public sources focus more on placement numbers than on day-to-day peer coding culture, so the culture itself remains only moderately evidenced.
positive_themes: Strong CSE placement outcomes and current technical clubs
Hackathons, coding contests and research projects are institution-published
negative_themes: Placement pages combine on-campus and off-campus selections
Non-CSE access to software roles and recruiter eligibility are not publicly documented

locality_and_student_life.summary: The permanent campus is close to the airport but about 19-25 km from major railway stations and central Guwahati. Public discussions often perceive the campus as relatively isolated, while official sources emphasize city access and regional travel opportunities.
positive_themes: App-based taxis and some buses serve the approach road
negative_themes: Distance from central Guwahati may limit spontaneous city access
Evening transport convenience is not sufficiently documented

overall_satisfaction.summary: Signals are mixed: academics, faculty and placements are common strengths, while campus life, infrastructure breadth and management are recurrent concerns in public discussions. The evidence is not strong enough to claim a college-wide satisfaction level or whether most students would choose it again.
positive_themes: Permanent green campus and current technical facilities
negative_themes: First-year accommodation uncertainty for some boys
Management criticism in a recent anonymous account

parent_mode_summary.summary: IIIT Guwahati is a statutory Institute of National Importance with verified JoSAA admission, a current 2026 seat matrix, published four-year tuition progression and recent placement statistics. Families should budget for rising tuition and possible first-year off-campus PG accommodation for some boys. Scholarships are external and not guaranteed.
priority_points: Calculated four-year hosteller estimate at current rates is INR 15.51 lakh, subject to hostel/mess revisions
All girls and PwD students are promised on-campus accommodation; some boys may be placed in empanelled PGs
Airport access is excellent; major railway stations are 19-25 km away
Placement snapshots mix on-campus and off-campus selections and have pending results
Current medical/emergency coverage and exact hostel timings need confirmation
parent_mode_summary.source_confidence: High for identity, seats, cutoffs and published fees; medium for placement scope and facilities; low-to-medium for qualitative experience.

student_mode_summary.summary: Best suited to students prioritising CSE/ECE/ECAI, research exposure and technical placements over a large-city-centre or large-university campus experience. The technical ecosystem is credible, while student reports on fest quality, management and day-to-day campus life are mixed.
priority_points: Common first year and official branch-change pathway after two semesters
Strong published CSE placement outcomes; ECE outcomes are more variable
Technical clubs include programming, robotics, ML and developer communities
Campus is green and near the airport but relatively far from central Guwahati
Some first-year boys may commute from empanelled PG accommodation

decision_risks.evidence: Admission Batch 2026 tuition totals INR 13 lakh before hostel, mess and other costs.
Approximately 80-120 boys may need external empanelled PG accommodation.
The placement-cell page and JoSAA profile report different 2025-26 overall denominators and averages.

decision_risks.risk: New ECAI programme lacks historical cutoff and placement record
decision_risks.evidence: ECAI appears in the 2026 seat matrix, while historical 2023-25 cutoffs contain only CSE and ECE.
The institute states it does not provide scholarships on its own.
Official sources list 19 clubs and major events, while several public student discussions describe limited campus life.
Current NAAC, NBA, AICTE and UGC records were not conclusively verified.

data_gaps: Final current NAAC grade/validity, NBA programme validity, AICTE approval record and UGC category were not conclusively verified.
The exact final 2025-26 placement cohort, domestic/international classification of highest packages, internship/PPO counts and stipends are unavailable.
Recruiter-specific roles, branch eligibility, CGPA requirements, selected counts and packages are generally not published.
Current scholarship amounts, income ceilings, deadlines and renewal rules require scheme-level verification.
Exact current laundry facilities, banking/ATM, medical staffing, ambulance availability and official hostel curfew are not verified.
Latitude/longitude, nearest major hospital and detailed public-transport timings require map/manual verification.
Club-specific latest event dates and recruitment processes are not consistently published.
ECAI has no 2023-25 historical cutoff or placement outcomes because it is new in the 2026 matrix.
